LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT JOHN HANSEN'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY FOR LIMITED PURPOSES OF DEPOSING THE PERSON(S) MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE OF PLAINTIFFS' BUSINESS AND TO COMPEL DEPOSITION(S) IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO APRIL 14, 2016 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 BECAUSE THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION PRIOR TO THE DEADLINE FOR ISSUANCE OF A TENTATIVE RULING. THE JUDGE PRO TEM HAS STATED THAT THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION WILL BE PROVIDED ON MONDAY. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For Limited Purposes Of Deposing The Person(S) Most Knowledgeable Of Plaintiffs Business And To Compel Deposition(S)
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Igleheart of Rouda, Feder, Tietjen & McGuinn telephone number 415-398-5398 for Plaintiffs Andrew Housey and Elizabeth Housey.
Owen Rooney of Edrington, Schirmer & Murphy LLP telephone number925-827-3300 for Defendant John Hansen.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant John Hansen's Motion To Reopen Discovery For Limited Purposes Of Deposing The Persons Most Knowledgeable Of Plaintiffs Business And To Compel Depositions is denied. Mr. Hansen has not shown sufficient need and reasons for the PMK deposition or that he acted diligently to take that deposition. Plaintiffs' Counsel is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim pursuant to CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Nancy Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 818-551-7300. (302/HK)
